title: feat: Change host from meaningless 'd' to given hostname from --url parameter when using tcp scheme
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

### Background

A request to podman REST API is used character 'd' as a hostname.
When it will add it from --url parameter dynamically, we will be able to handle request via host header.
For example, we can use nginx as a reverse proxy and use host header as consistent hashing seed.

### Question

I can't find some of test code for `pkg/bindings/connection.go` .
Which should I add test for this changes?

Thanks.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *takutakahashi* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **umohnani8** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @umohnani8` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@takutakahashi "d" was used as the host name since it is a placeholder. The name is never resolved and connections via the ssh tunnel never expose the name. For this change to be made, all API tests that do not use tcp:// will need to be updated.
exactly what @jwhonce said, it could be `foorbar` or `podman` and the behavior would not different.  This is true for similar RESTfull APIs including docker.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@takutakahashi what do you want us to do with this PR?

I couldn’t work for this PR.
I’m very sorry to close it.
